message:
fix(fr-protocol): sanitize CRLF in inline RESP frame bodies

SimpleString/Error frames are inline-terminated by the first \r\n on
the wire, so any embedded \r or \n in the body terminates the frame
early and lets bytes after the split be re-parsed by the peer as a
separate reply. Many production call sites (e.g.
RespFrame::Error(format!("ERR Unrecognized option '{attr}'")) where
attr is a client-controlled argv token) feed user-controlled bytes
into the body, making this a CRLF-injection / frame-smuggling
primitive on malformed argv.

Match upstream Redis' _addReplyErrorFormat sanitization, which does
sdsmapchars(s, "\r\n", "  ", 2): replace \r and \n with spaces while
encoding so the on-wire frame is always well-formed. The new
push_inline_sanitized helper keeps the fast path (no \r/\n present)
zero-copy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
